title: Overtime Calculator
name: Overtime Calculator
metaDescription: Estimate overtime pay using regular hourly wages, standard hours, overtime hours, and an overtime multiplier. Review weekly pay and annualized earnings assumptions in one place.
intro: Estimate how overtime hours can change a weekly paycheck using your regular hourly pay, standard hours, overtime hours, and overtime multiplier.
shortDescription: Estimate weekly overtime pay and annualized earnings using regular pay, overtime hours, and multiplier assumptions.
longDescription: This overtime calculator helps you estimate gross overtime pay under a simplified U.S.-oriented overtime model. It is useful when you want to understand how extra hours may change weekly earnings, compare overtime-heavy schedules with standard hourly work, or annualize a recurring overtime pattern before moving into broader salary planning.
problemSolved: Helps estimate how overtime hours may change gross pay under a weekly work schedule.

inputs.label: Regular Hourly Pay
inputs.explanation: Enter the base hourly wage before any overtime premium is applied.

inputs.label: Standard Hours
inputs.explanation: Use the number of non-overtime hours in the week or pay scenario you want to model.

inputs.label: Overtime Hours
inputs.explanation: Enter the extra hours paid at the overtime rate in the same week or pay scenario.

inputs.label: Overtime Multiplier
inputs.explanation: Use 1.5 for time-and-a-half or change it if your overtime policy uses a different multiplier.

inputs.label: Weeks Per Year
inputs.explanation: Use 52 only if you expect this overtime pattern to continue all year. Reduce it for seasonal or inconsistent overtime.

outputs.label: Regular Pay
outputs.explanation: Gross pay from standard hours before overtime premium is added.

outputs.label: Overtime Rate
outputs.explanation: Hourly overtime pay after applying the multiplier.

outputs.label: Overtime Pay
outputs.explanation: Gross pay generated by overtime hours in this scenario.

outputs.label: Total Pay
outputs.explanation: Combined gross pay from standard and overtime hours for the modeled week.

outputs.label: Total Hours
outputs.explanation: Total hours worked across standard and overtime time.

outputs.label: Effective Hourly Rate
outputs.explanation: Average gross hourly value across all hours in the scenario.

outputs.label: Annualized Pay
outputs.explanation: Estimated gross yearly pay if this weekly pattern continues for the weeks per year you entered.

formula.label: Overtime Pay Formula
formula.expression: Total Pay = (Regular Hourly Pay x Standard Hours) + ((Regular Hourly Pay x Overtime Multiplier) x Overtime Hours)
formula.plainText: The calculator estimates regular pay from standard hours, calculates an overtime rate from the multiplier, adds overtime pay, and then annualizes the total if you choose to model recurring overtime.

formulaExplanation: Regular pay is calculated by multiplying your base hourly wage by the standard hours in the scenario.
Overtime rate is calculated by multiplying the base hourly wage by the overtime multiplier, such as 1.5 for time-and-a-half.
Overtime pay is calculated by multiplying the overtime rate by the overtime hours.
Total pay is the sum of regular pay and overtime pay, and annualized pay assumes this pattern repeats for the number of weeks you enter.
methodologyNotes: This calculator is a simplified overtime estimator for planning and comparison, not payroll software.
It does not determine legal overtime eligibility, exemption status, local labor-law rules, or employer-specific overtime policies.
The annualized result is only useful if the same overtime pattern repeats consistently across the weeks you model.
Shift differentials, double-time rules, bonuses, and taxes are not included in the calculation.
If you want to compare this gross pay pattern with salary-style earnings, use the hourly-to-salary or salary-to-hourly tools after reviewing the result.

example.scenario: An hourly worker earns $28 per hour, works 40 standard hours, adds 8 overtime hours at time-and-a-half, and expects that pattern across 50 weeks this year.
example.resultSummary: The calculator estimates $1,120 in regular pay, an overtime rate of $42, about $336 in overtime pay, total weekly gross pay of about $1,456, and annualized gross pay of about $72,800.

resultInterpretation.defaultText: Use the weekly total and annualized pay as planning estimates only. Real overtime earnings depend on eligibility rules, schedule consistency, and employer payroll policies.

thresholds.label: Overtime Hours
thresholds.interpretation: A recurring overtime pattern in this range can materially change gross earnings and may be worth comparing against salary offers or take-home pay targets.

warningMessages: If overtime is irregular or seasonal, the annualized result may overstate or understate what you actually earn over a full year.

faq.question: How is overtime pay calculated?
faq.answer: Overtime pay is calculated by multiplying the regular hourly pay by the overtime multiplier, then multiplying that overtime rate by the overtime hours worked.

faq.question: What does a 1.5 overtime multiplier mean?
faq.answer: A 1.5 multiplier means time-and-a-half. If the regular hourly rate is $20, the overtime rate becomes $30 per hour.

faq.question: Does this calculator determine whether I legally qualify for overtime?
faq.answer: No. This tool estimates pay only. It does not determine legal overtime eligibility, exemption status, union rules, or employer-specific payroll treatment.

faq.question: Why does the calculator ask for weeks per year?
faq.answer: That assumption is used only to annualize the weekly overtime pattern. If overtime is not consistent all year, lower the weeks-per-year input so the estimate stays realistic.

faq.question: How should I use this with the other salary calculators?
faq.answer: Use the overtime calculator first when extra hours are part of your pay pattern. Then compare the annualized gross figure with hourly-to-salary, salary-to-hourly, or gross-to-net salary tools depending on the next question you want to answer.

disclaimer: This overtime calculator provides estimate-based gross pay projections using the assumptions you enter. It does not determine legal overtime eligibility, exemption status, taxes, union rules, double-time policies, or employer-specific payroll treatment.
assumptions: Uses a simplified weekly overtime model based on regular hourly pay, standard hours, overtime hours, overtime multiplier, and weeks worked per year. It estimates gross pay only and assumes the entered overtime pattern repeats across the annualization period.
